summary: Venom stage decomp work centered on src/melee/gr/grvenom.c and ground variable typing. The PR added or improved C for several grVenom routines, got grVenom_80204428 from 99.70% to a full match, and raised grVenom_80206874 from 0.00% to 82.52%. The key structural change was expanding grVenom_GroundVars and adding a grVenom_GroundVars2 overlay in types.h so code could address JObj pointers and xE0 bitfield state directly instead of fake casts through unrelated ground structs or ad hoc local typedefs. The automated report also recorded a regression in grVenom_802053B0 from 95.38% to 82.77%, so the net lesson is useful but not purely monotonic.

key_files.role: Main Venom stage C changes; matched grVenom_80204428, improved grVenom_80206874, adjusted matching tactics and typed accesses in multiple routines.
Expanded grVenom_GroundVars with gp+F0 through gp+100 fields and added grVenom_GroundVars2 overlay for JObj pointers and xE0 bitfield state.
Updated grVenom_80205E84 prototype from Vec2* to Vec3*.

what_changed: Removed the explicit fake-match comments around grVenom_80204428 and switched its setup from gp->gv.venom casts plus mutecity2 bitfield abuse to gp->gv.venom2 typed JObj pointer fields and xE0_state bitfield access.
Added PAD_STACK(8) in grVenom_80204428, with local padding additions in grVenom_80204284, indicating stack/register-shape tuning for matching.
Deleted the local GroundVenom_ForLighting typedef in grvenom.c and rewrote grVenom_80204B88 to use Ground* plus gp->gv.venom2.xE0_state.xE0_state_pad.state for lighting/environment state.
Changed grVenom_802052E0 local declaration order and simplified the zero-vector fallback to pos->x = pos->y = pos->z = grVe_804DB740.
Changed grVenom_80205E84 and its header declaration to take Vec3* instead of Vec2*, removing casts from callers in grVenom_802053B0.
Replaced several raw pointer-offset writes at gp+F0, gp+F4, gp+F8, gp+FC, and gp+100 with named grVenom_GroundVars fields xF0, xF4, xF8, xFC, and x100.

Added a C body for grVenom_80206874 outside the prior disabled block, including animation setup, child/other ground object linkage, scale setup through HSD_JObjSetScaleX/Y/Z, and initialization of Venom ground variables.
In types.h, moved and expanded grVenom_GroundVars, added offset comments, and added grVenom_GroundVars2 to the Ground union alongside venom.
smart_moves: Solved the grVenom_80204428 fake match by modeling the actual overlapping ground-variable layout instead of borrowing mutecity2 bitfields at the same offset.

Used a second Venom-specific ground-vars overlay, grVenom_GroundVars2, to represent the same gp+C4 region as HSD_JObj* entries plus xE0 bitfields without destroying the existing numeric grVenom_GroundVars view used by other functions.
Removed a one-off local lighting struct and centralized the xE0 bitfield knowledge in types.h, making future Venom functions reuse the same layout evidence.
Promoted known gp+F0..gp+100 accesses into named struct fields, reducing brittle byte-offset casts in grvenom.c.
Updated function signatures and call sites together for grVenom_80205E84, allowing Vec3* callers to be typed directly rather than cast through Vec2*.
Kept explicit offset comments in the struct definitions, which helps reconcile gp-relative assembly offsets with C field names during future matching.
decomp_lessons: If a stage-specific GroundVars region is reused with different interpretations across routines, add a stage-specific overlay in the Ground union rather than casting through an unrelated stage's struct.
Bitfields embedded in ground variable blobs should be named at the real offset; this avoids fake matches based on layout-compatible but semantically wrong structs.
Local typedefs that only exist to satisfy one function are a sign that the shared header layout may be incomplete; moving the layout to types.h can unlock cleaner matches elsewhere.
Direct field names for gp-relative values such as gp+F0 or gp+100 make later decomp work safer than repeated pointer arithmetic.
Small stack-shape changes such as PAD_STACK and local declaration ordering can be decisive for closing near-matches.
A PR can improve one function while regressing another; decomp.dev metrics should be checked per-symbol, not only by total matched-code percentage.
naming_conventions: Stage ground variable structs follow gr<Stage>_GroundVars, here grVenom_GroundVars and grVenom_GroundVars2.
Unknown fields retain offset-style names such as xC4, xD8, xF0, and x100, with comments documenting gp-relative offsets.
The secondary overlay name venom2 is used in the Ground union when the same stage data needs an alternate typed interpretation.
The xE0 bitfield wrapper is named xE0_state, with a nested xE0_state_pad view for the multi-bit state field and b0..b7 aliases for individual flag bits.
Temporary object relationship variables in the new grVenom_80206874 body use generic names like other and other_gp rather than overclaiming semantic names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Inserted unused padding locals in grVenom_80204284, likely to affect stack/register allocation.
Changed declaration order in grVenom_802052E0 by moving Vec3 jobj_pos before pointer locals.
Replaced manual assert/rotation dirtying code in grVenom_802053B0 with HSD_JObjSetRotationY(jobj, grVe_804DB740).
Used repeated reloads of grVe_804D6A30 before HSD_JObjSetScaleX/Y/Z in grVenom_80206874, matching the visible diff pattern rather than hoisting the attr read.
Used typed fields and unions to remove casts such as *(s32*)&gp->gv.venom.xD8 and casts of Vec3* to Vec2*.

review_feedback.feedback: Matched code increased by 284 bytes overall for GALE01, from 65.02% to 65.03% as reported in the PR comment.
New full match: main/melee/gr/grvenom grVenom_80204428 improved from 99.70% to 100.00%.
Unmatched improvements included grVenom_80206874 from 0.00% to 82.52%, .sdata2 from 10.17% to 19.53%, and grVenom_80204284 from 97.58% to 97.74%.
Regression noted for grVenom_802053B0, dropping from 95.38% to 82.77%.
No human review comments or PR body were present in the provided slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching grVenom_802053B0 to see whether the 82.77% regression was recovered.
Search for other Ground union overlays named *2 that model alternate interpretations of the same gp+C4 ground variable region.
Look for follow-up work that gives semantic names to grVenom_GroundVars xF0, xF4, xF8, xFC, and x100.
Check whether grVe_804D6A30 later receives a typed attr struct with fields at offsets 0x2C and 0x34.
Search for other stage lighting functions using xE0 bitfield state to reuse the grVenom_80204B88 layout pattern.
